sglang.title: fix bug of `undefined is_single` in meth `create_abort_task`
sglang.author: wcsjtu
sglang.bodyExcerpt:
Motivation
According to the current logic in GenerateReqInput.post_init, when neither text nor input_ids are provided, the is_single field will not be dynamically set. This results in an AttributeError being triggered in TokenizerManager.create_abort_task.
Additionally, dynamically setting fields in

sglang.title: Support MiniCPM3
sglang.author: Achazwl
sglang.bodyExcerpt:
Motivation
Add support for the new model MiniCPM3-4B

Modifications
I added a new file in python/sglang/srt/models called minicpm3.py.
The main difference between the old minicpm.py and the newly added minicpm3.py is LongRope and MLA.
The MLA implementation is basically the same as DeepSeek-V2.

Che

sglang.title: Fix CORS compatibility with OpenAI, vLLM, TGI, LMDeploy
sglang.author: josephrocca
sglang.bodyExcerpt:
Motivation
Fixes #333
Fix CORS compatibility with OpenAI API, vLLM, TGI, LMDeploy, TabbyAPI, and others. Currently SGLang is the only one which does not use wildcard CORS headers. This makes it annoying to make requests directly from a web page to a SGLang server, since it requires adding an extra p

sglang.title: [Minor] move triton attention kernels into a separate folder
sglang.author: merrymercy

sglang.title: Deprecate --disable-flashinfer and introduce --attention-backend
sglang.author: merrymercy
sglang.bodyExcerpt:
We plan to build better abstraction around the attention backends, so we can easily switch between them and share the upper-level scheduling and optimizations (e.g., cuda graph).
We will use --attention-backend triton and --attention-backend flashinfer instead of --disable-flashinfer.

vllm.title: [Bugfix] Fix missing `post_layernorm` in CLIP
vllm.author: DarkLight1337
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
CLIP encoder currently assumes that len(self.encoder.layers) < config.num_hidden_layers which may not always be the case.
This is a follow-up to #8106.
It additionally fixes a potential issue in SigLIP where the weights are not sharded like in CLIP. cc @Isotr0py Fixed in #8269

vllm.title: [CI/Build] enable ccache/scccache for HIP builds
vllm.author: dtrifiro
vllm.bodyExcerpt: We are currently not using ccache and sccache for HIP builds, see the docs

vllm.title: [Frontend] Clean up type annotations for mistral tokenizer
vllm.author: DarkLight1337
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
The expected inputs and outputs for mistral tokenizer are different from HF's tokenizers. So, in this PR I have split them into different functions to avoid introducing many union types.
cc @patrickvonplaten since you originally worked on using mistral tokenizer in vLLM

vllm.title: [Bugfix] Ensure multistep lookahead allocation is compatible with cuda graph max capture
vllm.author: alexm-redhat
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
This PR fixes the issue described here: #8068.
The problem is that multistep lookahead block allocation may allocate more blocks than what max capture is using for the cuda graphs. The solution here is to simply ignore these extra additional blocks, since the model won't execute more than the max ca

vllm.title: [CI/Build][Kernel] Update CUTLASS to 3.5.1 tag
vllm.author: tlrmchlsmth
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Now that CUTLASS 3.5.1 is officially out, use the v3.5.1 tag instead of the commit that we were on.
This has the benefit of letting us use GIT_SHALLOW=TRUE again, which should speed up fetching CUTLASS.

vllm.title: [Hardware][NV] Add support for ModelOpt static scaling checkpoints.
vllm.author: pavanimajety
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
This change adds support for a new quantization class that has the
ability to load ModelOpt checkpoints through HF. It can be invoked via -
llm = LLM(model=model_path, quantization="modelopt")
The checkpoint needs a hf_quant_config.json for loading the right
quantiztion format.

vllm.author: TKONIY

vllm.title: [Frontend] Create ErrorResponse instead of raising exceptions in run_batch
vllm.author: pooyadavoodi
vllm.bodyExcerpt: The run_batch API currently creates error responses for requests that fail inside the engine. Error responses appear in the output file indicating which request ID has failed. This PR makes sure error responses are also created for requests that fail due to invalid url endpoints or unsupported cases
